data.title: BPCL arm takes full control of Brazil oil and gas venture for Rs 2,312 crore
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited ( (IN:BPCL) ).

Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited has completed the acquisition of full control of IBV Brasil Petroleo Limitada through its indirect wholly owned subsidiary, BPRL Ventures BV. BPRL Ventures BV increased its stake in IBV from 60.86% to 100% by purchasing an additional 39.14% shareholding for Rs. 2,312 crore in a cash transaction that closed on 1 July 2026.

IBV, incorporated in Brazil in 2005, holds participating interests in oil and gas concessions in the country but reported no turnover in 2023, 2024 and 2025. The deal, cleared by DIPAM and NITI Aayog, is aimed at providing BPCL with additional equity oil and gas from Brazilian assets, reinforcing India’s energy security and strengthening BPCL’s upstream portfolio in the global oil and gas sector.

More about Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited

Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited is a Government of India enterprise and one of the country’s leading integrated oil and gas companies. It is active across refining, marketing and upstream exploration, with international ventures such as BPRL Ventures BV supporting its strategy to secure energy supplies for the Indian market.

Average Trading Volume: 520,148

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: 1319.8B INR

data.title: M Vest Water Grants 645,000 Share Options Under Long-Term Incentive Plan
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

M Vest Water AS ( (DE:7YA) ) has shared an announcement.

M Vest Water has allocated 645,000 share options to executive management and key employees under its long-term incentive program, in line with terms approved by shareholders. Of this, 300,000 options went to primary insiders, including CFO Morten Hilton Thomassen and COO Henrik Meland Madsen, with each option giving the right to subscribe for one share at a strike price of NOK 5.55.

The options vest in three equal tranches annually from 2027 to 2029 and expire three years after each vesting date, bringing the total outstanding options in the company to 1,115,000. The move underscores M Vest Water’s use of equity-based incentives to align management and key staff with shareholder interests as it pursues growth in the water treatment industry.

More about M Vest Water AS

M Vest Water AS is an environmental technology company focused on developing non-toxic, biodegradable water treatment products and solutions for both industrial and municipal markets. Listed on Euronext Growth under the ticker MVW, the company targets high levels of purification through cost-efficient systems designed to minimize environmental impact.

Average Trading Volume: 29,767

An announcement from NatWest Group ( (GB:NWG) ) is now available.

NatWest Group has disclosed that its chair and several non‑executive directors, including the senior independent director, purchased modest quantities of ordinary shares on 1 July 2026 on the London Stock Exchange under the company’s shareholding policy. The coordinated insider purchases, all executed at £6.6780 per share, signal board‑level alignment with shareholders and may be viewed as a vote of confidence in the bank’s prospects and governance discipline.

The transactions involve multiple persons discharging managerial responsibilities, reinforcing compliance with UK Market Abuse Regulation requirements on transparency of director dealings. While the volumes are relatively small, such regular policy‑driven acquisitions help underpin market trust in NatWest’s oversight framework and provide stakeholders with greater visibility into directors’ financial commitment to the group.

data.title: Rockhopper ties executive options to three-year shareholder return test
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Rockhopper Exploration ( (GB:RKH) ) has issued an update.

Rockhopper Exploration has granted new share options to senior executives under its 2025 Long Term Incentive Plan, aligning management rewards with shareholder returns. Chief executive Sam Moody received options over 997,044 shares and chief financial officer William Perry was awarded 703,013 options, all structured as nominal-cost options.

The awards will vest based on total shareholder return achieved over a three-year period to June 2029, with full vesting requiring at least a 15% compound annual return and no vesting below 5%. The structure underscores the company’s intention to tie executive remuneration to sustained share price performance, introducing a one-year post-vesting holding period and up to seven years to exercise vested options, which may influence management incentives and investor perceptions of governance.

data.title: China Yuchai International Declares US$0.87 Cash Dividend for FY2025
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Hong Leong Asia ( (SG:H22) ) has provided an announcement.

China Yuchai International, listed on the NYSE, operates through its principal subsidiary Guangxi Yuchai Machinery Company Limited, one of China’s leading powertrain solutions manufacturers. Founded in 1951, Yuchai designs, manufactures and sells light-, medium- and heavy-duty engines for commercial vehicles, construction and agricultural machinery, marine applications and power generation, offering diesel, natural gas and new-energy powertrains including electric, hybrid and fuel cell systems.

The company serves auto OEMs and distributors via a broad sales and service network across China and globally, and in 2025 sold 461,309 engines, generating revenue of RMB 24.6 billion. China Yuchai International has declared a cash dividend of US$0.87 per ordinary share for the financial year ended December 31, 2025, with payment scheduled for July 28, 2026 to shareholders of record on July 17, 2026.

The payout underscores the company’s ability to return cash to investors following a year of substantial engine sales and multi‑billion renminbi revenues, signaling confidence in its operational performance and ongoing positioning in China’s competitive powertrain market. The dividend decision may be viewed by shareholders as a sign of management’s positive assessment of cash generation and balance sheet strength amid evolving demand for conventional and new‑energy powertrain solutions.

The most recent analyst rating on (SG:H22) stock is a Buy
with a S$4.28 price target.

data.title: Aceso Life Science Delays Dispatch of Subscription Agreement Circular to Shareholders
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited ( (HK:0474) ) has shared an announcement.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited has announced a further delay in sending a shareholder circular related to a Subscription Agreement and the proposed issue of consideration shares under a specific mandate. The circular, which will include reports from an independent board committee and an independent financial adviser, was originally expected by 30 June 2026.

The company now expects to dispatch the circular on or before 31 July 2026, citing the need for additional time to prepare and incorporate required information. While the postponement may slightly extend the timetable for shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting, it underscores the company’s emphasis on providing comprehensive disclosure and independent evaluation to its investors.

More about Aceso Life Science Group Limited

Aceso Life Science Group Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 00474. The board currently consists of two executive directors, one non-executive director and three independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical governance structure for a Hong Kong-listed life science group.

The company operates within the broader life sciences sector, focusing on transactions and capital arrangements that require shareholder scrutiny and independent advice. Its activities are subject to Hong Kong listing rules, necessitating formal circulars and extraordinary general meetings for key agreements such as subscription arrangements.

data.title: ContiOcean Redirects HK Listing Proceeds Toward R&D Partnerships and Vessel Platform
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest announcement is out from Contiocean Environment Tech Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2613) ).

ContiOcean Environment Tech Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-based environmental technology company listed in Hong Kong via H shares, focusing on marine-related systems and equipment. The Group develops prototype products such as low-flashpoint fuel supply systems for ammonia, carbon capture solutions and waste heat recovery systems, supported by dedicated R&D staff and cooperative research with universities and institutions.

The company leverages an ocean-going vessel as a maritime R&D and mobile exhibition platform to test and showcase its equipment and pipeline products, aiming to bolster its position in the maritime environmental technology segment. Its market focus lies in ship-based environmental technologies that help the marine industry improve energy efficiency and reduce emissions.

ContiOcean Environment Tech Group has announced a change in the allocation of approximately RMB119 million in unutilised net proceeds from its Hong Kong global offering. The board has rebalanced planned spending among R&D projects, recruitment and cooperative development, while confirming that the acquisition of a controlling stake in a vessel-based R&D and exhibition platform remains on track, now expected to complete by end-July 2026.

The revised plan trims funding earmarked solely for hiring 13 new R&D staff, extends the recruitment timeline to end-2026, and redirects part of the proceeds to cooperative R&D with universities, enterprises and research institutions through 2027. These shifts suggest a more flexible capital deployment strategy that could enhance innovation capacity and partnerships, while slightly delaying some talent expansion, with potential implications for the pace of product development and stakeholder expectations on growth execution.

More about Contiocean Environment Tech Group Co., Ltd. Class H

ContiOcean Environment Tech Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-based environmental technology company listed in Hong Kong via H shares, focusing on marine-related systems and equipment. The Group develops prototype products such as low-flashpoint fuel supply systems for ammonia, carbon capture solutions and waste heat recovery systems, supported by dedicated R&D staff and cooperative research with universities and institutions.

EAM Solar ASA ( (DE:EA20) ) has issued an update.

EAM Solar ASA has launched a NOK 40–55 million rights offering and, following a positive ruling from the Milan Court of Appeal on the second arbitration decision, issued a supplemental prospectus dated 30 June 2026. The updated document is available via Norne Securities, while the subscription period, trading of rights, and key settlement dates remain unchanged.

Investors who subscribed before the supplemental prospectus was published may withdraw their subscriptions until 6 July at 16:30 CEST under applicable prospectus regulations. The court decision and prospectus update clarify the company’s legal position without altering the timeline of the capital raise, providing greater certainty for shareholders and participants in the rights issue.

data.title: POSCO Holdings Unveils Triple-Core Growth and Capital Strategy at 2026 CEO Investor Day
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An announcement from POSCO ( (PKX) ) is now available.

On July 2, 2026, POSCO Holdings outlined a mid- to long-term portfolio transformation and financial strategy at its CEO Investor Day in Seoul. The group plans a “Triple-Core” portfolio built around Industrial, Strategic and Energy Resources, targeting overseas crude steel capacity of 10 million tons by 2031, lithium production of 173,000 tons by 2033 to rank among the top five global producers, and a broader footprint in rare earths, specialty gases, LNG and renewable energy.

Management set 2028 financial goals of KRW 87.9 trillion in consolidated revenue and KRW 6.7 trillion in operating profit, supported by KRW 29.1 trillion in investment from 2026 to 2028. POSCO also announced a shareholder value-focused capital policy, aiming for a 35–40% payout of adjusted net income via dividends and buybacks, reducing holding-company discounts by monetizing some listed subsidiaries, and targeting a price-to-book ratio of at least 1.0x by 2028, signaling a bid to re-rate the stock and strengthen its strategic positioning in global resource markets.

Spark’s Take on PKX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PKX is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (thin margins, low ROE, and negative free cash flow) and bearish technicals (price below major DMAs with negative MACD and very weak RSI/Stoch). Valuation is also a headwind due to the high P/E, partially tempered by a moderate dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on PKX stock,
click here.

More about POSCO

POSCO Holdings Inc. is a South Korea-based industrial group rooted in steelmaking and increasingly focused on critical materials and energy. The company supplies crude steel globally, with expansion plans in high-growth markets such as India and the U.S., and is building positions in lithium, battery materials, rare earths, specialty gases, LNG and renewables to become a national supplier of critical resources.

Average Trading Volume: 330,735

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $15B
